title: prunella vulgaris subsp. lanceolata
intro: Prunella vulgaris subsp. lanceolata, also known as the lance-leaved selfheal or self-heal, is a subspecies of the self-heal plant that is characterized by its lance-shaped leaves. It is a flowering plant that is native to Europe and Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Prunella vulgaris subsp. lanceolata include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as having potential anti-cancer and anti-viral effects. Prunella vulgaris subsp. lanceolata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
